source: Command Doctor Mission Studio visual registry

assets.title: Devices sharing a service
assets.caption: Two endpoints reach a shared booking service through a local switch and gateway.
assets.alt_text: Laptop and tablet connected through a switch and gateway to a booking service.
assets.text_alternative: A network is shown as end devices connected to a switch, then a gateway, then a service. The important evidence is that devices exchange useful information through links.
evidence_requirements: Identify at least two connected devices.
Identify the shared service the devices are trying to reach.
Explain that the links carry requests and responses.

assets.title: Request and response path
assets.caption: A terminal asks for a service and receives a useful response.
assets.alt_text: Point of sale terminal sending a request to a payment service and receiving an approval response.
assets.text_alternative: The terminal asks for a service, the network carries the request, the server answers, and the terminal can continue. Communication has a purpose and a direction.
evidence_requirements: Identify the requesting device.
Identify the responding service.
Explain why the response matters to the user.

assets.title: LAN, WAN, and internet scope
assets.caption: Local, provider, and internet boundaries are separated for troubleshooting scope.
assets.alt_text: Local office LAN connected through a WAN or ISP path to an internet service.
assets.text_alternative: The LAN is the local room or site, the WAN links sites or providers, and the internet reaches public services. The same symptom can have different scope.
evidence_requirements: Separate a local printer or room issue from an internet issue.
Identify the boundary between local network and provider path.
Explain why scope changes the next evidence to collect.

assets.title: Simulation versus production boundary
assets.caption: A local simulator is visually separated from real production impact.
assets.alt_text: Local simulated switch separated from a production network by a clear safety boundary.
assets.text_alternative: The local simulator is safe practice space. A production switch affects real guests, staff, and services, so the learner must keep that boundary clear.
evidence_requirements: Identify which side is local simulation.
Identify which side is production impact.
Explain why a first attempt belongs in simulation.

assets.title: Generic 24-port switch front
assets.caption: Preview of the future beginner switch visual language.
assets.alt_text: Generic 24-port Ethernet switch front view with grouped ports and uplink area.
assets.text_alternative: The generic switch preview shows rows of Ethernet ports and an uplink area without claiming a real vendor model. It previews how a future lesson will introduce switch hardware.
evidence_requirements: Identify front-panel Ethernet ports.
Identify that the image is generic and not a vendor model.
Explain that this preview does not complete Level 1.

assets.title: Annotated switch ports and LEDs
assets.caption: Preview labels for ports, LEDs, uplink, and console areas.
assets.alt_text: Generic switch front panel annotated with port, LED, uplink, and console labels.
assets.text_alternative: The annotated preview labels the parts a beginner will later inspect: access ports, link LEDs, an uplink area, and a console marker. It is a visual contract, not a completed lesson.
evidence_requirements: Point to an access port group.
Point to link status LEDs.
Explain uplink and console labels as future learning targets.

assets.title: Switch in endpoint topology
assets.caption: Preview of endpoints connecting through a switch to a shared service.
assets.alt_text: Two endpoints connected to a generic switch, with the switch forwarding toward a gateway.
assets.text_alternative: The topology preview shows the switch as the local connection point between endpoints and the next network hop. It prepares a future switch lesson without claiming authored coverage.
evidence_requirements: Identify endpoints connected to switch ports.
Identify the switch as a local forwarding point.
Explain that the future lesson still needs authored evidence.

assets.title: Healthy link versus disconnected link
assets.caption: Preview comparison for link-light evidence.
assets.alt_text: Side-by-side generic switch ports showing one healthy connected link and one disconnected link.
assets.text_alternative: The comparison preview shows how a future lesson can connect link-light evidence to a healthy or disconnected endpoint. It is a visual contract only.
evidence_requirements: Identify the healthy connected link.
Identify the disconnected link.
Explain that link-light evidence needs confirmation before changes.
